backup_api_paths: GET / (S3 ListAllMyBuckets - returns ListAllMyBucketsResult XML when anon ListBuckets is allowed)
GET /<bucket>/?list-type=2 (S3 ListObjectsV2 - returns ListBucketResult XML of object keys)
GET /<bucket>/?acl (read bucket ACL, reveals AllUsers/AuthenticatedUsers grants)
GET /<bucket>/?location (LocationConstraint - reveals which storage-location/backend cloud holds the data)
ai_scope_discriminator: Confirm AI/ML payload from NAMES ONLY (bucket names + top-level object key prefixes), never object bodies. (1) Anon GET / returns ListAllMyBucketsResult; read <Bucket><Name> values - flag buckets named models, model-registry, mlflow, mlflow-artifacts, artifacts, checkpoints, ckpt, datasets, training-data, train, embeddings, vectors, weights, huggingface, hf-cache, sagemaker, kubeflow, feast, dvc, lakefs, wandb, tensorboard, onnx, safetensors. (2) Anon GET /<bucket>/?list-type2&max-keys=50&delimiter=/ returns ListBucketResult; read only <Key> / <CommonPrefixes><Prefix> values - ML tells in the manifest: 0_/MLmodel/conda.yaml/model.pkl/python_model.pkl prefixes (MLflow run layout), *.safetensors *.ckpt *.pt *.pth *.onnx *.gguf *.bin extensions, config.json+tokenizer.json+pytorch_model.bin trio (HuggingFace snapshot), .dvc/ or data.dvc (DVC), _delta_log/ (Delta/lakeFS-style), feature_store/ (Feast). Key prefix structure proves AI content; do not GET the object. As a multi-cloud S3 front the LocationConstraint (?location) tells you whether the named ML data physically lives in aws_s3/azure/gcp/local - record the backend, do not pull it.

dorks.version: "Server: ScalityS3" "APN/1.0 Scality/1.0 Scality CloudServer for Zenko"

fingerprint_markers: HTTP response header Server: ScalityS3 (vendor-unique; hardcoded by the Arsenal HTTP layer, distinct from AmazonS3/MinIO/Ceph)
Node.js S3-protocol implementation listening on tcp/8000 (S3 API), tcp/8443 (TLS)
x-amz-request-id and x-amz-id-2 headers present on a non-AWS-IP host with Server: ScalityS3
productName string APN/1.0 Scality/1.0 Scality CloudServer for Zenko (appears in S3 multipart/copy responder identity)
Internal daemon ports 9990 (socket.io metadata) and 9991 (REST data) co-located on the host
REMOTE_MANAGEMENT / Zenko Orbit management-agent behavior on the deployment
Default demo credentials accessKey1 / verySecretKey1 still accepted (signed-request confirmation only, out of passive scope)
fp_guards: The ListBucketResult xmlns http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/ is the GENERIC S3 namespace - shared by AWS S3, MinIO, Ceph RGW, Garage, SeaweedFS. It does NOT identify Scality on its own. Require Server: ScalityS3 to attribute.

Server header disambiguation: AWS S3 = AmazonS3, MinIO = MinIO, Ceph RGW = Ceph, Garage = Garage. Only ScalityS3 = this platform. A bare port 8000 hit is NOT Scality (8000 collides with restic-rest, lakeFS, Django, ComfyUI, many dev servers).

Server: ScalityS3 is the same header used by Scality RING / S3 Connector (commercial) and ARTESCA - the header alone does not distinguish OSS Zenko CloudServer from the commercial appliance; treat as Scality-family, narrow with the Zenko productName string or Orbit tells.

A 200 with ListAllMyBucketsResult must contain at least one <Bucket><Name> child to count as anon-listable; an empty <Buckets/> or a 403 AccessDenied / InvalidAccessKeyId XML body = surface open, anon listing NOT exercised.

Honeypot/poisoning guard: a Server: ScalityS3 header on a host whose body is HTML (not XML) or whose S3 error XML uses non-Scality <Code> wording is a spoofed banner; require a well-formed S3 XML error or list body.
pivot_paths: GET / -> ListAllMyBucketsResult: enumerate bucket NAMES only, classify ML vs non-ML by name
GET /<bucket>/?list-type=2&max-keys=20&delimiter=/ -> top-level key prefixes only (manifest read, no object GET)
GET /<bucket>/?acl -> read ACL to confirm whether AllUsers/AuthenticatedUsers grant is what made it anon-listable (root-cause evidence)
GET /<bucket>/?location -> LocationConstraint reveals backing cloud (aws_s3/azure/gcp/local) without touching data
GET /<bucket>/?versions&max-keys=20 -> version history of ML artifact keys (names/timestamps only)
GET /<bucket>/?uploads -> in-flight multipart uploads (large checkpoint/dataset transfers), names only
Favicon / TLS cert CN pivot on the same host to attribute operator; cross-reference 9990/9991 exposure for metadata-plane reachability (observe only)
restraint_note: Names-only. Read bucket names, object KEY prefixes, ACL grantees, LocationConstraint, and version/upload listings - these metadata reads ARE the finding and prove AI/ML content. Do NOT GET any object body (that is exfiltration of model weights / datasets / training data). Do NOT issue PUT/POST/DELETE, do NOT change bucket policy or ACL, do NOT create or delete buckets, do NOT use the default accessKey1/verySecretKey1 to perform any write. A 403/InvalidAccessKeyId on listing = surface open, access not exercised; log it as such, never as an actionable read.
